Package io.forja.components.utilities.fxIcon

Class FxIcon

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.shape.Shape
javafx.scene.text.Text
org.kordamp.ikonli.javafx.FontIcon
io.forja.components.utilities.fxIcon.FxIcon
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, org.kordamp.ikonli.javafx.Icon
public class FxIcon extends org.kordamp.ikonli.javafx.FontIcon
A styled icon component built on top of Ikonli's FontIcon.

FxIcon renders glyphs from any Ikonli icon pack on the classpath (Feather is bundled by default — add others like ikonli-material-pack to use additional sets). Forja adds a semantic color variant system that binds to the active theme, and a fluent builder API.

The preferred way to construct an FxIcon is via the builder:

     
      FxIcon save = FxIcon.builder()
          .literal("fth-save")
          .size(20)
          .variant(SemanticVariant.ACCENT)
          .build();

Constructor and direct instantiation are also supported for simpler cases:

     
      FxIcon save = new FxIcon("fth-save");

Since FxIcon extends Ikonli's FontIcon, every Ikonli API remains accessible — FontIcon.setIconColor(javafx.scene.paint.Paint), FontIcon.setIconSize(int), and so on can still be used directly if you need to escape the Forja variant system.

Unlike FxButton and FxLabel, FxIcon does not extend a JavaFX Control — it's a Text subclass. Tooltips and disabled are not exposed on the builder because they don't apply to text nodes; wrap the icon in a Forja control if you need those behaviors.

See Also:
  • SemanticVariant
  • FxIcon.Builder

  • Property Details

    • variant

      public javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<SemanticVariant> variantProperty
      Returns the variant property of this icon.

      The variant controls the color of the icon via the active theme. It can be observed or bound like any standard JavaFX property:

           
      icon.variantProperty().addListener((obs, old, val) -> ...)
      See Also:
      • getVariant()
      • setVariant(SemanticVariant)

  • Constructor Details

    • FxIcon

      public FxIcon()
      Creates an FxIcon with no glyph and the default variant (SemanticVariant.DEFAULT).

    • FxIcon

      public FxIcon(String iconLiteral)
      Creates an FxIcon with the given Ikonli icon literal and the default variant (SemanticVariant.DEFAULT).
      Parameters:
      iconLiteral - the Ikonli icon literal, e.g. "fth-save"

    • FxIcon

      public FxIcon(String iconLiteral, SemanticVariant variant)
      Creates an FxIcon with the given Ikonli literal and variant.
      Parameters:
      iconLiteral - the Ikonli icon literal, e.g. "fth-save"
      variant - the semantic color variant - see SemanticVariant

  • Method Details

    • variantProperty

      public javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<SemanticVariant> variantProperty()
      Returns the variant property of this icon.

      The variant controls the color of the icon via the active theme. It can be observed or bound like any standard JavaFX property:

           
      icon.variantProperty().addListener((obs, old, val) -> ...)
      See Also:
      • getVariant()
      • setVariant(SemanticVariant)

    • getVariant

      public SemanticVariant getVariant()
      Returns the current variant of the icon.
      Returns:
      the current SemanticVariant, never null

    • setVariant

      public void setVariant(SemanticVariant v)
      Sets the variant of this icon.
      Parameters:
      v - the desired SemanticVariant, must not be null

    • builder

      public static FxIcon.Builder builder()
      Returns a new FxIcon.Builder for constructing an FxIcon with a fluent API. 
      
  FxIcon save = FxIcon.builder()
      .literal("fth-save")
      .size(20)
      .variant(SemanticVariant.ACCENT)
      .build();
      Returns:
      a new FxIcon.Builder instance
